text: kinda weird, Ronin .” “I found Tabitha .” The words hung in the air, suspended like the ever-present haze in the sky . “What? Where is she? Is she okay? Did you talk — ” He raised his hands, signaling her to stop. She did, though the excitement didn’t fade from her expression. He ignored the electric tingle on his cheek . “She’s dead , Lara .” The color drained from her face, and she stared at him blankly. “That’s a fucking lie .” “I found her on the way into town, with a dismantled synth .” “You’re lying!” she yelled, jumping to her feet. She slapped him, sending a brief, dull throb of pain through his left shoulder . He rose and caught her arms before she struck him again. Lara struggled against his hold as he took both her wrists into one hand, clamping them together . “You’re a lying can of shit! All you fucking bots can go fuck each other and rust that way !” “She had a scar,” he said gently. With one finger, he traced a line from the first knuckle of her left pinky to her thumb . Lara gave a final, surprisingly strong tug on her arms, and then crumpled. Her wail filled his audio receptors. She sagged against him, sobbing against his chest . Ronin released her wrists and — though he wasn’t sure why — wrapped his arms around her. It wasn’t a tight embrace, but it was solid, and she leaned into him. Her body shook, her teardrops tracing wet paths down his abdomen . Didn’t she want to know how? Hadn’t she wanted all the details ? No . That information would only cause more pain. It was enough for her, now, that Tabitha was dead. The cause wasn’t important, the circumstances couldn’t alleviate the grief . “I buried her.” He brushed a palm over her soft hair. “West of town. Away from… all this .” Though she didn’t speak, her sobs quieted. Her breathing was erratic; sharp, shuddering inhalations rocked her body with no predictable pattern . “Now, Tabitha is free,” he continued. “Free from the struggles we all suffer every day. She doesn’t have to worry about…where her next meal will come from, or if she’ll have a roof over her head, or whether a cut will get infected. She can just … rest .” Lara pulled back and looked up at him. Her eyes were red, her cheeks wet, her gaze searching. It reminded him so much of that day in the rain; she’d been lost, nearly broken, but not yet defeated . She leaned forward, not looking away, and pressed her mouth to his . Ronin first registered the warmth of her lips, and then their softness. They yielded to shape of his own, molding to a perfect fit. An electric pulse, unlike anything he’d experienced, spread across his face. This was no malfunction, not a result of an error in his coding . His eyes widened as the sensation crackled over every electrode in his body. His attraction for her, his arousal, surged with new power, triggering the automated systems that pumped fluid into his phallus. It swelled in his pants . Pulling back again, Lara glanced at his lips. Her pink tongue slipped out of her mouth for an instant before she kissed him once more . * * * L ara closed her eyes, waiting for the wave of revulsion, for the surge of panic when she realized that she was kissing a bot . It didn’t come . His lips were surprisingly warm and not nearly as rigid as she thought they’d be. He slid his arms around her, caging her against his chest. Her heart raced, and her breath was short, but it wasn’t because of fear. It was because of his nearness, his touch . Reluctantly, she broke contact again, tilting her head back to study his face. This wasn’t Warlord, wasn’t a random bot. He was Ronin; patient, considerate, trustworthy Ronin . Lara didn’t want to think about what her sister had suffered, didn’t want to feel the heartbreak of losing the only person she’d ever loved. Her life had been pain for as long as she could remember; the pain of hunger, of loneliness, of never being good enough to deserve all that Tabitha had done for her. It gathered in her now, twenty-three years of struggle and uncertainty, writhing in her stomach and constricting her chest . Scattered over time, she could handle it. She was strong enough for that. But all that suffering, balled together, would break her. Beneath the weight of her despair, she wanted to live. She just needed to escape before it crushed her…needed the feelings only Ronin could instill in her. Needed to feel safe. Cared for . Loved ? Anything but acknowledge the reality; she was now truly alone . “Make me forget,” she whispered, lip quivering. “I can’t… I don’t want to think. Not now .” Ronin didn’t hesitate. He lifted her like she weighed nothing, covering her mouth in a kiss that stole her breath even though he didn’t have lungs, and laid her on the bed. It creaked as he climbed atop it and settled between her thighs, propping himself up on an arm. The dizzying speed of his movement stopped as abruptly as it had begun, and his touch became a sensual caress . He gently wiped the moisture from her face, and then trailed his hand along the curve of her jaw, following it to the slender column of her neck. His hand moved down until his fingers brushed the collar of her shirt. Heat blossomed everywhere his skin met hers, pulsing with each beat of her heart . She placed her palms on his chest.
